title: scan_tx updates witnesses for spent notes, growing witnesses map unboundedly
description: scanner.rs scan_tx() calls witness.append(node) for ALL entries in self.witnesses on every Sapling output processed. self.witnesses is loaded from all unspent notes in load_state(). Spent notes are removed from the notes table but their entries remain in self.witnesses for the entire lifetime of the scanner process (they are never removed from the in-memory map after send_payment marks them spent). Over time self.witnesses grows to include every note ever received. On each block each output triggers O(total_notes_ever) append operations. This is the nie-en5f.2 class of bug (witnesses not purged on spend). The DB save_block_state correctly overwrites witness rows but does not delete rows for spent notes. The in-memory witnesses map is never pruned. Trigger: wallet that has received and spent many notes. Bad behavior: scan_block time grows linearly with lifetime note count; eventually memory exhaustion.
